questions.question_text: For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have what?
What was the name of the first man created by God?
The Sermon on the Mount is found primarily in which Gospel?
Hebrews 11:1 defines faith as the assurance of things hoped for and the what of things not seen?
Who was the man commanded by God to build an ark to save his family from a global flood?
In the Old Testament, the first five books are collectively known as the Pentateuch or the what?
According to Galatians 5:22-23, what is the first fruit of the Spirit listed?
In the story of creation, on which day did God create the sun, moon, and stars?
Which New Testament book immediately follows the four Gospels?
Romans 3:23 states that all have sinned and fall short of the what of God?
Which Old Testament figure was sold into slavery by his jealous brothers and later became a high official in Egypt?
What event marked the beginning of the Christian church, where the Holy Spirit descended on the disciples?
Colossians 1:17 says that in Christ, all things hold what?
Who was the younger brother of Moses who acted as his spokesman to Pharaoh?
How many books are there in the New Testament?
Philippians 4:6 instructs believers to not be anxious about anything, but in everything, by prayer and what, to present requests to God?
Which king of Israel was known for his wisdom and his building of the First Temple in Jerusalem?
What is the shortest book in the New Testament by verse count?
Psalm 119:105 says that God's word is a lamp to my feet and a what to my path?
Who was the woman who gave birth to Jesus?
The letters of Paul that were written while he was imprisoned are known as the what Epistles?
1 Corinthians 15:14 states that if Christ has not been raised, then our preaching is useless and so is your what?
In the book of Judges, who was the hero who defeated the Midianites with only 300 men?
Which Gospel writer was a doctor and historian who also wrote the Book of Acts?
John 4:24 states that God is Spirit, and his worshipers must worship in spirit and in what?
Which prophet was thrown into a lion's den but survived?
The last book of the Bible, which contains prophecy about the end times, is named what?
Jeremiah 32:27 declares, 'I am the LORD, the God of all mankind. Is anything too what for me?'
Who was the first king of Israel?
How many plagues did God send upon Egypt before Pharaoh released the Israelites?
2 Corinthians 4:18 tells us to fix our eyes not on what is seen, but on what is what?
What was the name of the mountain where Moses received the Ten Commandments?
The Book of Lamentations, traditionally attributed to Jeremiah, mourns the destruction of which city?
Ephesians 6:11 instructs believers to put on the full what of God?
The man who walked with God and was later taken by God without experiencing death was whom?
Which New Testament book, written by Paul, is famous for its emphasis on justification by faith alone?
James 1:5 promises that if anyone lacks wisdom, they should ask whom, who gives generously to all without finding fault?
What was the name of the man who married Naomi's daughter-in-law Ruth?
Which Gospel is known for having a unique focus on Jesus' parables, especially the parable of the Prodigal Son?
Colossians 3:15 instructs believers to let the what of Christ rule in their hearts?
In the Old Testament, who was the prophet taken to heaven in a whirlwind by a chariot of fire?
Which book of the New Testament begins with the words: 'In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God'?
Jesus summarized the Law and the Prophets into two great commandments: love God and love your what?
Which of the 10 Commandments deals with honoring one's parents?
The Book of Acts records the missionary journeys of the Apostle Paul and whom else, who was Peter's right-hand man?
Ephesians 2:8 states that we are saved by grace through what, and this is not from yourselves, it is the gift of God?
Which Old Testament man was tested by God, being asked to sacrifice his son Isaac?
The Book of Hebrews emphasizes the superiority of Christ's priesthood, comparing it to the Old Testament figure whom?
Proverbs 12:22 teaches that the LORD delights in those who are what?
What was the initial job/occupation of the Apostle Peter before he followed Jesus?
Which book of the Old Testament is a poetic dialogue dealing with the complexity of human suffering and divine justice?
In the Beatitudes, Jesus said, 'Blessed are the what, for they will be shown mercy'?
Who was the Old Testament prophet whose wife was unfaithful to him, serving as a metaphor for Israel's relationship with God?
Which of Paul's letters contains the famous 'love chapter' (Chapter 13)?
Isaiah 41:10 says, 'So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. I will what you and help you...'
The Israelites celebrated their freedom from slavery in Egypt with an annual festival called the what?
The Book of James is often referred to as a book of practical Christian what?
Acts 4:12 states that salvation is found in no one else, for there is no other what given under heaven by which we must be saved?
What was the name of the garden where Jesus prayed before his arrest?
The Old Testament book of Psalms is primarily a collection of what?
Titus 3:8 teaches believers to be careful to devote themselves to doing what?
Which king of Israel was known as 'a man after God's own heart'?
The Old Testament book of Nehemiah primarily describes the rebuilding of what?
options: D. The Ark of the Covenant

questions.question_text: Philippians 4:7 promises that the peace of God, which transcends all what, will guard your hearts and minds in Christ Jesus?
Who was the son of Abraham and Sarah, born in their old age?
How many tribes of Israel were there in total?
John 15:5 says, 'I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much what?'
In the Old Testament, who was the prophet whose scroll was publicly cut up and burned by King Jehoiakim?
Which Gospel writer was a former tax collector, called by Jesus from his customs post?
1 Corinthians 12:4-6 explains that there are different kinds of gifts, but the same what?
What was the name of the place where Jesus was born?
The Book of Ruth is categorized in the Old Testament as which type of book?
James 1:13 states that when tempted, no one should say, 'God is tempting me,' because God cannot be tempted by what, nor does he tempt anyone?
Which prophet was thrown into a pit/cistern but later rescued and continued to preach?
The majority of the book of Psalms is traditionally attributed to which author?
Lamentations 3:22-23 says that the Lord's great love is new every what?
What was the physical object that Moses used to part the Red Sea?
Which of Paul's letters contains the famous 'Christ Hymn' describing Jesus' humiliation and exaltation?
Romans 12:12 tells believers to be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, and faithful in what?
Who was the man who inherited Elijah's mantle and performed twice as many miracles as his predecessor?
The Book of Revelation was written by the Apostle John while he was exiled on which island?
Psalm 133:1 praises how good and pleasant it is when God's people live together in what?
Who was the first person to commit murder, according to the Bible?
The Book of Haggai, a minor prophet, urged the returned exiles to focus on rebuilding what structure?
James 3:8 warns that the tongue is a restless what, full of deadly poison?
The city of Jerusalem was often referred to by what name, meaning 'City of David'?
Which New Testament letter contains a list of requirements for church officers (elders and deacons)?
Matthew 6:33 encourages believers to seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be what to you?
What was the name of the man who led the Israelites into the Promised Land after Moses died?
Which Gospel ends with Jesus instructing the disciples to wait in Jerusalem for the Holy Spirit before beginning their mission?
In the Great Commission (Matthew 28:18), Jesus stated that all what in heaven and on earth has been given to him?
What was the name of the prostitute in Jericho who hid the Israelite spies?
The Book of Ecclesiastes famously concludes that the whole duty of man is to fear God and keep what?
Romans 4:3 asks what the Scripture says concerning Abraham, answering that he believed God, and it was credited to him as what?
Who was the man who killed his brother Abel?
The Book of Ezekiel contains the famous vision of the valley of dry what?
The 'Fruit of the Spirit' listed in Galatians 5:22-23 includes love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, gentleness, and what else?
What was the name of the city that was destroyed by fire and brimstone, from which Lot and his family fled?
The Book of Romans was written by the Apostle Paul to the believers in the city of what?
1 Peter 2:9 states that believers are a chosen race, a royal priesthood, a holy nation, and a people belonging to whom?
Who was the woman who became queen of Persia and saved her people from genocide?
Which prophet is known for prophesying that the Messiah would be born in Bethlehem?
Hebrews 12:1 encourages believers to run with perseverance the what marked out for us?
What was the name of the man whose strength was tied to his hair?
Which Gospel writer opens their book with a detailed genealogy of Jesus, tracing his lineage through King David to Abraham?

theme: The Church as the Body of Christ

questions.question_text: 1 Corinthians 12:27 states that believers are the what of Christ, and each one of you is a part of it?
What type of food did God provide to the Israelites in the desert after they complained about having no food?
The Old Testament book of Exodus primarily deals with the Israelites' freedom from what?
2 Corinthians 9:7 teaches that God loves a cheerful what?
The Book of Judges primarily covers the period of Israel's history between Joshua and the establishment of the what?
Which Apostle wrote the Book of Revelation?
Jeremiah 31:33 describes the New Covenant as God putting his law into their minds and writing it on their what?
What was the name of the city where the Apostle Paul was converted on the road?
The Book of Leviticus is primarily concerned with laws, sacrifices, and the what of the Israelite people?
Psalm 90:12 asks God to teach us to number our days, that we may gain a heart of what?
Who was the Old Testament prophet that publicly rebuked King David for his sin with Bathsheba?
The 'Book of the Twelve' (or Minor Prophets) begins with which prophet?
Romans 1:16 states that the Gospel is the power of God for salvation to everyone who does what?
Which of the Old Testament patriarchs was named Israel, which means 'he struggles with God'?
Which Gospel focuses heavily on Jesus' identity as the Son of God, using seven 'I Am' statements?
The greatest demonstration of love, according to John 15:13, is laying down one's life for whom?
Who was the Old Testament prophet who was a shepherd and a fig-picker before God called him?
The Book of Numbers primarily documents the Israelites' journey and struggles in what location?
Psalm 135:6 declares that the LORD does whatever he pleases in the heavens, on the earth, and where else?
options: B. The seas and all the depths

questions.question_text: Which New Testament writer penned the shortest book in the Bible, warning against false teachers?
The biblical hero Samson was known for his extraordinary what?
Galatians 5:1 states: 'It is for what that Christ has set us free'?
Who was the mother of Samuel, who prayed earnestly for a son?
Which of the four Gospels begins with a focus on Jesus' eternal existence as the 'Word' (Logos)?
According to 1 Corinthians 1:10, what quality should believers exhibit that prevents divisions?
options: B. Perfect agreement in mind and thought

questions.question_text: The Jewish council that tried Jesus and condemned him was known as the what?
The Book of Habakkuk opens with the prophet complaining to God about what problem?
The Book of Colossians strongly emphasizes the superiority and headship of whom over the entire universe and the church?
The story of Esther recounts how she saved the Jewish people from the plot of the wicked minister named what?
What type of food did the Israelites eat in the wilderness that was supplied by God?
The foundational truth of the Book of Proverbs is that the beginning of knowledge is the what?
What was the occupation of the Apostle Peter before he followed Jesus?
Which book of the Old Testament contains the famous story of a contest between God's power and human wealth/pride?
James 4:10 instructs believers to humble themselves before the Lord, and in return, he will do what?
Who was the first person to see the resurrected Jesus, according to the Gospel of John?
The name of the priest who blessed Abraham after a battle and received a tithe from him was what?
Philippians 1:11 describes the 'fruit of righteousness' as coming through whom?
Who led the Israelites into the Promised Land after Moses died?
Which prophet was thrown into a den of lions and miraculously delivered?
Matthew 6:7 instructs that when you pray, you should not keep on doing what, like pagans do?
What was the name of the high priest who questioned Jesus and presided over the Sanhedrin trial?
Which Old Testament book contains the famous lines: 'For everything there is a season, and a time for every matter under heaven'?
In Revelation 21:4, God promises to wipe away every what from their eyes in the New Jerusalem?
The two apostles known as 'Sons of Thunder' (Boanerges) were James and whom?
The Book of Haggai is one of the post-exilic minor prophets that urged the people to rebuild what?
The Book of Hebrews argues that Jesus is superior to the Old Testament system, including the Law, the prophets, and whom else?
Which king of Judah was miraculously granted 15 extra years of life after praying to God?
The 'Golden Rule' of Christian ethics, stated by Jesus, is to treat others as you would want whom to treat you?
Romans 10:9 states that if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be what?
Who was the female military leader who collaborated with Barak to defeat the Canaanite army led by Sisera?
The Book of Joel in the Old Testament warns of the coming 'Day of the Lord' using the imagery of what natural disaster?
1 Timothy 4:8 states that physical training is of some value, but what kind of training is of value in all things?
What was the name of the place where Jesus was crucified?
The Book of Malachi, the last book of the Old Testament, strongly condemns the priests for neglecting what?
In John 6:35, Jesus declares, 'I am the Bread of Life.' He says those who come to him will never what?
What was the name of the place where the disciples were first called Christians?
The Book of Deuteronomy is structured largely as a series of three farewell speeches given by whom?
In the New Testament, what event is commonly understood as a sign that Jesus' ministry was approved by the Holy Spirit?
Which major prophet lived through the Babylonian exile and wrote letters of encouragement to the exiles?
1 John 4:18 says that 'perfect love drives out what?'
Acts 1:8 promises that believers will receive power when whom comes upon them, making them witnesses?
What was the name of the pool in Jerusalem where Jesus healed a paralyzed man who had been ill for 38 years?
The Book of Hosea uses the metaphor of an unfaithful marriage to describe God's relationship with whom?
In the Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24-25), Jesus teaches that no one knows the day or hour of his return, except for whom?
Who was the wealthy Old Testament man whose patience was tested by Satan and the loss of his family and property?
Which letter of Paul is primarily concerned with addressing false teachings about Christ and the resurrection?
The Book of Hebrews argues that Jesus serves as our eternal high priest, in the order of whom, rather than the Levitical priesthood?
The 'Shema' (Deuteronomy 6:4), the central confession of Jewish faith, begins by declaring: 'The LORD our God, the LORD is what?'
Who was the king whose heart was hardened by God, leading to the series of plagues on Egypt?
John 8:12 records Jesus declaring, 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in what?'
What was the name of the river where the prophet Ezekiel received his extraordinary visions of God's glory and a new temple?
The Book of Titus gives instructions to the young church leader Titus concerning the proper ordering and selection of what church offices?
Matthew 5:16 tells believers to let their light shine before others so that they may see your good deeds and glorify whom?
The central message of the prophet John the Baptist was a call to what?
The historical books of the Old Testament end with the accounts of the Israelites returning from exile and rebuilding under Ezra and whom?
1 Corinthians 10:13 states that God will not let you be tempted beyond what you can bear, and with the temptation, he will also provide what?
What was the name of the place where Jesus told his disciples to wait for the Holy Spirit after his ascension?
Which book of the Old Testament is largely composed of the sayings and wise observations of King Solomon?
Isaiah 40:31 promises that those who hope in the LORD will renew their what, soaring like eagles?
What type of tree did Zacchaeus climb to see Jesus as He passed by?
The Book of 1 Samuel primarily covers the transition of Israel's leadership from the Judges to which new form of government?
Ephesians 5:1 urges believers to be what of God, as dearly loved children?
What miracle of Jesus is recorded in all four Gospels?
Who was the female missionary associate of the Apostle Paul often mentioned as being 'in the Lord' and a fellow worker?
2 Timothy 3:16-17 states that all Scripture is God-breathed and useful for teaching, rebuking, correcting, and what else?
Who was the Old Testament prophet whose life was miraculously preserved in a burning fiery furnace?
What is the collective name for the letters written by James, Peter, John, and Jude that are not written by Paul?
Romans 8:28 promises that God works all things together for what, for those who love him and are called according to his purpose?
What was the first city the Israelites conquered after crossing the Jordan River into the Promised Land?
Which Gospel focuses heavily on Jesus' suffering and is thought to be written primarily to a Roman audience?
1 Peter 4:10 teaches that each believer should use what they have received to serve others, as faithful what of God's grace?
Who was the prophet known for his elaborate prophecies and warnings about the destruction of Jerusalem, often conveyed through symbolic actions?
Which disciple was known for being scholarly and was the only Gentile author in the New Testament?
Revelation 12:11 states that the saints 'overcame him [Satan] by the blood of the Lamb and by the word of their what?'
Who was the major Old Testament prophet that foretold the Messiah would be a suffering servant?
Which island did Paul and Barnabas visit first on their initial missionary journey?
2 Corinthians 10:4 reminds believers that the weapons we fight with are not the weapons of the world, but have what kind of power to demolish strongholds?
The Book of Jonah serves as a primary example of God's compassion for what people group?
The doctrine of **original sin** traces its biblical origin to the actions of whom?
James 5:16 teaches that the prayer of a righteous person is powerful and what?
Who was the Moabite woman who chose to follow her mother-in-law Naomi back to Bethlehem and became an ancestor of David?
Which letter of Paul strongly combats the belief that Gentiles must be circumcised and follow the Law of Moses to be saved?
Philippians 2:5-8 encourages believers to have the same mindset as Christ Jesus, who humbled himself and became what?
The Book of Revelation refers to Jesus by what title, meaning the first and the last?
In the Old Testament, the tribe of **Levi** was designated specifically for what hereditary task?
Jesus taught in John 14:6, 'I am the way, and the truth, and the what?'
Who was the first high priest of Israel, and Moses' brother?
The Book of Amos condemns Israel and surrounding nations primarily for what kind of sin?
options: B. Social injustice and oppression of the poor

questions.question_text: Galatians 3:29 states that if you belong to Christ, then you are Abraham's what, and heirs according to the promise?
Who was the king that had a feast where handwriting mysteriously appeared on the wall, predicting his downfall?
Which Gospel focuses heavily on the theme of 'perfect prayer' and includes the most material about Jesus' ministry in Perea and Judea?
Colossians 3:2 instructs believers to 'set their minds' on things above, not on what?
The Book of Joshua details the conquest of the Promised Land, starting with the battle of what city?
Which Old Testament book contains the famous scene where God speaks to a prophet in a 'gentle whisper' rather than a loud display?
Romans 5:3-5 teaches that suffering produces what key spiritual quality that leads to hope?
In the New Testament, who was the first Christian martyr, stoned to death after preaching a sermon?
The Book of Philemon is a personal letter primarily dealing with the relationship between a slave owner and his runaway slave named what?
Ephesians 2:20 describes the church as being built on the foundation of the apostles and whom?
What was the name of the garden where Adam and Eve were placed before the Fall?
Which minor prophet is known for his emphasis on holiness and a vision of the coming Messianic kingdom?
The opening verses of the Book of Revelation state that the book is the revelation of whom?
Who was the female military leader and temptress who manipulated Samson to discover the secret of his strength?
What did Jesus use to cleanse the temple (twice in his ministry), overturning the tables of money changers?
options: D. An appeal to the crowd

questions.question_text: Psalm 100:4 encourages believers to 'Enter his gates with thanksgiving and his courts with what?'
Which Old Testament book details the Israelites' settlement and establishment in the land of Canaan, including the allocation of tribal territories?
The Book of 2 Corinthians is often seen as Paul's defense of his what, against challenges from false apostles?
Romans 5:1 states that since we have been justified through faith, we have what with God through our Lord Jesus Christ?
What was the name of the wealthy, secretive member of the Sanhedrin who defended Jesus and helped bury him?
The Book of Nahum is primarily a prophecy against which Assyrian city, predicting its total destruction?
James 2:26 asserts that faith without what is dead?
Who was the Old Testament prophet whose main message was 'The righteous shall live by his faith'?
Which of Paul's letters is written to a church battling over the resurrection of the dead and spiritual gifts?
Matthew 26:41 encourages believers to 'Watch and what,' so that they do not fall into temptation?
The Book of Deuteronomy contains a re-telling and explanation of what key Old Testament events and laws?
options: B. The Exodus and Wilderness Journey

questions.question_text: In the New Testament, who was the Roman centurion in Caesarea, known for his devotion and piety, whose household converted after Peter's visit?
Jesus taught in Mark 10:45 that the Son of Man did not come to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a what for many?
Who was the younger brother of Moses and Aaron who assisted them in their ministry?
The Book of Obadiah is the shortest book in the Old Testament and is a prophecy primarily against which nation?
In John 3, Jesus tells Nicodemus that no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again of water and what else?
The covenant God made with Abraham involved a promise of descendants, land, and what else?
Which of the four Gospels is known for its detailed genealogical record tracing Jesus' ancestry back to Adam?
Ephesians 3:19 prays that believers may grasp the love of Christ and be filled to the measure of what?
options: A. All the fullness of God

questions.question_text: Who was the woman who killed the Canaanite general Sisera by driving a tent peg through his temple?
The Book of Job primarily attempts to address the challenging theological question of what?
options: D. The best form of government

questions.question_text: In the Old Testament, the **Tabernacle** and later the **Temple** served as the central place of what activity?
What was the name of the place in the Temple where the high priest entered only once a year on the Day of Atonement?
The first sermon recorded in the Book of Acts (on the Day of Pentecost) resulted in how many people being baptized?
In his letters, Paul often refers to the *ekklesia*, the Greek term for what organized group of believers?
Which New Testament book is addressed to Gaius, urging him to show hospitality to traveling missionaries?
The covenant God made with King David promised that what would be established forever?
In Luke 9:23, Jesus tells his followers that if anyone would come after him, he must take up his what daily and follow him?
What was the name of the prostitute in Jericho who helped the Israelite spies and was saved when the city fell?
The Book of Ecclesiastes is traditionally attributed to which wise king?
Hebrews 4:14-16 assures us that we have a high priest (Jesus) who is able to do what, because he was tempted in every way, just as we are?
What was the central artifact contained within the Ark of the Covenant?
options: B. The Tablets of the Covenant (Ten Commandments)

questions.question_text: Which prophet's book is famous for containing 70 'weeks' that point to the timing of the Messiah's arrival and the end of time?
In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus taught that a good tree cannot bear bad fruit, nor can a bad tree bear good fruit. This relates to the quality of one's what?
Who was the Old Testament prophet known for his dramatic confrontation with 450 prophets of Baal on Mount Carmel?
The term **'Apostle'** primarily refers to a person who has been what?
options: B. Sent out with a commission

questions.question_text: Ephesians 4:3 urges believers to make every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit through the bond of what?
Who was the Old Testament figure who famously wrestled with God/an angel at the river Jabbok and had his name changed to Israel?
Which of the following is considered a 'Pastoral Epistle' of Paul?
Revelation 21:1 foretells the creation of a new heaven and a new earth, where the first heaven and earth had passed away and there was no longer any what?
In the Old Testament, who was the righteous man whom God translated (took up to heaven) without him dying?
The Sermon on the Mount begins with a series of teachings on the character traits and blessings of believers, known as the what?
Galatians 4:4 says that God sent his Son when the fullness of what arrived, to redeem those under the law?
What was the name of the valley where David defeated the giant Goliath?
The Book of Zephaniah primarily focuses on the description of the coming 'Day of the Lord' and calls for a remnant to seek what?
In Matthew 11:28, Jesus invites those who are weary and burdened to come to him, promising he will give them what?
Who was the Roman governor of Judea who heard Paul's defense and was 'almost persuaded' to become a Christian?
Which New Testament book is a letter primarily dealing with joy and gratitude despite persecution and imprisonment?
The Book of Matthew concludes with Jesus commissioning his disciples to go and make disciples of all what?
What was the name of the place where Jesus was arrested after his period of fervent prayer?
Which book is the first of the four major divisions of the Old Testament, containing the creation accounts?
2 John 1:10 warns against receiving into your house or greeting anyone who does not bring the true teaching (doctrine) of whom?
Who was the king who built the first permanent Temple for God in Jerusalem?
The central theme of the Book of **Titus** is the requirement for Christians to live what kind of lives, especially church leaders?
Proverbs 29:18 famously states that 'Where there is no prophetic vision (or revelation), the people what?'
In the New Testament, who was the tax collector who left his booth to follow Jesus and later wrote a Gospel?
Which historical book recounts the splitting of the united Kingdom of Israel into the northern and southern kingdoms?
Ephesians 2:7 states that God raised us up in Christ to show the incomparable what of his grace in the coming ages?
Who was the Old Testament prophet that was swallowed by a great fish after trying to flee from God's command to preach to Nineveh?
Which New Testament book is concerned with Jesus' relationship to the Sabbath, divorce, and the coming judgment?
Romans 8:16 states that the Spirit himself testifies with our spirit that we are what of God?
Which of the following books is NOT classified as one of the major prophetic books?
What was the initial sign/miracle that confirmed the apostles' authority and the arrival of the Holy Spirit on the Day of Pentecost?
1 Thessalonians 2:12 encourages believers to live lives worthy of God, who calls them into his own what and glory?
In the Old Testament, the name **Elohim** is most commonly translated as what?
Who was the son of David and Bathsheba who became the successor to David's throne?
The final verse of the entire Bible (Revelation 22:21) concludes with a blessing of the grace of whom upon all?
What does the phrase **'Hallelujah'** mean?
What common item was the Apostle Paul working with when he earned a living on his missionary journeys?
